research_question: How does street network affect asthma at the census_tract level?

candidates.title: Street Network and Asthma

display.display_title: How Does Street Network Connectivity Relate to Asthma at the Census Tract Level?
display.research_question: At the US census tract level, is street network connectivity associated with asthma outcomes after controlling for sociodemographic confounders?
display.rationale: Linking street network connectivity to asthma at the census tract grain offers a policy-actionable lens: small-area variation in built-environment features is hypothesized to drive measurable differences in population health, and both inputs are publicly available.
display.contribution_angle: Combines OSMnx_OpenStreetMap with CDC_PLACES at a finer grain than most prior work, using a cross sectional spatial association design with a associational claim.
display.execution_summary: Sources: OSMnx_OpenStreetMap + CDC_PLACES. Method: cross sectional spatial association. Grain: census tract.

candidates.research_question: How does street network affect asthma at the census_tract level?

mitigations.residential_self_selection: Include county/metro fixed effects and avoid causal claims.
mitigations.spatial_autocorrelation: Use clustered or spatially robust standard errors.
mitigations.exposure_measurement_error: Run sensitivity checks with alternative feature definitions.

candidates.brief_rationale: Assess whether street network is empirically associated with asthma using OSMnx_OpenStreetMap and CDC_PLACES.
